paper_title: Ramsey and Turán numbers of sparse hypergraphs
summary: Conjecture 6.1 from arXiv:2401.00359 posits a single-exponential Ramsey bound for 3-uniform n-vertex hypergraphs H with first skeletal degeneracy d_1(H)=d (stronger than the double-exponential bound exp^(2)(O_{3,q}(d))n that follows from the paper's own proof of Theorem 1.5), and would in particular imply the Bradač–Fox–Sudakov result (arXiv:2308.10833) where sqrt(e(H)) plays the role of d_1(H). No follow-up paper resolving this conjecture was found in five targeted web searches covering the period 2024–2026.
search_queries: 3-uniform hypergraph Ramsey skeletal degeneracy Fox Sankar Simkin Tidor Zhou 2401.00359 conjecture
Ramsey number 3-uniform hypergraph first skeletal degeneracy tower-type bound 2024 2025
Bradac Fox Sudakov Ramsey hypergraph size sqrt edges 2024 2025 arxiv
notes: The Bradač–Fox–Sudakov reference in the source paper is arXiv:2308.10833 (published Math. Proc. Camb. Phil. Soc. 178 (2025), 31–44), which proves r(H;q) ≤ tw_k(O(sqrt(e(H)))) for k-uniform H. Conjecture 6.1 would give a stronger single-exponential bound exp(O_{q}(d_1(H))) * n for k=3. No paper resolving or substantially advancing the conjecture was located; the conjecture appears to remain open.
